segments.text: See now, the place where we dwell with you is too small for us.
segments.speaker: Sons of the Prophets

segments.text: Please, let us go to the Jordan, and let every man take a beam from there, and let us make there a place where we may dwell.
segments.speaker: Sons of the Prophets

segments.text: So he answered,
Go.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Then one said,
Please consent to go with your servants.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And he answered,
I will go.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: So he went with them. And when they came to the Jordan, they cut down trees.
But as one was cutting down a tree, the iron ax head fell into the water;
Alas, master! For it was borrowed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So the man of God said,
Where did it fall?
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And he showed him the place. So he cut off a stick, and threw it in there; and he made the iron float.
Therefore he said,
Pick it up for yourself.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: So he reached out his hand and took it.
Now the king of Syria was making war against Israel; and he consulted with his servants,
My camp will be in such and such a place.
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: And the man of God sent to the king of Israel, saying,
Beware that you do not pass this place, for the Syrians are coming down there.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Then the king of Israel sent someone to the place of which the man of God had told him. Thus he warned him, and he was watchful there, not just once or twice.
Therefore the heart of the king of Syria was greatly troubled by this thing; and he called his servants and said to them,
Will you not show me which of us is for the king of Israel?
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: And one of his servants said,
None, my lord, O king; but Elisha, the prophet who is in Israel, tells the king of Israel the words that you speak in your bedroom.
segments.speaker: Syrian Servant

segments.text: So he said,
Go and see where he is, that I may send and get him.
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: And it was told him, saying, 'Surely he is in Dothan.'
Therefore he sent horses and chariots and a great army there, and they came by night and surrounded the city.
And when the servant of the man of God arose early and went out, there was an army, surrounding the city with horses and chariots.
Alas, my master! What shall we do?
segments.speaker: Elisha's Servant

segments.text: So he answered,
Do not fear, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Lord, I pray, open his eyes that he may see.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Then the Lord opened the eyes of the young man, and he saw. And behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha.
Strike this people, I pray, with blindness.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And He struck them with blindness according to the word of Elisha.
This is not the way, nor is this the city. Follow me, and I will bring you to the man whom you seek.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: But he led them to Samaria.
Lord, open the eyes of these men, that they may see.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And the Lord opened their eyes, and they saw; and there they were, inside Samaria!
My father, shall I kill them? Shall I kill them?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: You shall not kill them. Would you kill those whom you have taken captive with your sword and your bow? Set food and water before them, that they may eat and drink and go to their master.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Then he prepared a great feast for them; and after they ate and drank, he sent them away and they went to their master. So the bands of Syrian raiders came no more into the land of Israel.
And it happened after this that Ben-Hadad king of Syria gathered all his army, and went up and besieged Samaria.
And there was a great famine in Samaria; and indeed they besieged it until a donkey's head was sold for eighty shekels of silver, and one-fourth of a kab of dove droppings for five shekels of silver.
Then, as the king of Israel was passing by on the wall, a woman cried out to him,
Help, my lord, O king!
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: If the Lord does not help you, where can I find help for you? From the threshing floor or from the winepress?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: Then the king said to her,
What is troubling you?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: And she answered,
This woman said to me, 'Give your son, that we may eat him today, and we will eat my son tomorrow.'
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: So we boiled my son, and ate him. And I said to her on the next day, 'Give your son, that we may eat him'; but she has hidden her son.
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: Now it happened, when the king heard the words of the woman, that he tore his clothes; and as he passed by on the wall, the people looked, and there underneath he had sackcloth on his body.
God do so to me and more also, if the head of Elisha the son of Shaphat remains on him today!
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: But Elisha was sitting in his house, and the elders were sitting with him. And the king sent a man ahead of him, but before the messenger came to him,

Do you see how this son of a murderer has sent someone to take away my head? Look, when the messenger comes, shut the door, and hold him fast at the door. Is not the sound of his master's feet behind him?
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And while he was still talking with them, there was the messenger, coming down to him; and then the king said,
Surely this calamity is from the Lord; why should I wait for the Lord any longer?
segments.speaker: King of Israel
